channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: O jogo de interesses na CPI do INSS - O Assunto #1689
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court ruled 8-2 that Congress, not the Judiciary, has the authority to decide whether to extend the INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI), overturning a temporary injunction by Minister André Mendonça that had delayed the committee’s conclusion. The final report will be read and may be voted on Friday, after a months-long stalemate due to Senate President Davi Alcolumbre’s silence on the extension request. The situation highlights growing tensions between the Legislative and Judicial branches and the strategic use of CPIIs in election years to shape political narratives of heroes and villains.

item.title: CPMI do INSS: veja quais argumentos levaram o STF a derrubar decisão que prorrogava comissão
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) overturned by an 8-2 vote the individual decision of Minister André Mendonça that had extended the INSS CPMI, ruling that the matter is an internal congressional issue and that the petition for mandado de segurança failed to meet the legal requirements of a clear and certain right. The ruling followed the dissent of Minister Flávio Dino, joined by other justices, while Mendonça and Fux voted to maintain the extension.

item.title: “Treine, seu puto”, diz Lula sobre fazer exercícios para ficar em forma
item.description: President Lula responded to conspiracy theories about his age and physical ability, urging people to exercise and adopt healthy habits after being seen doing gymnastics in a video filmed by First Lady Janja. He also emphasized the importance of prostate exams for men and said he would accompany Janja on her routine checkups, including breast cancer screenings, to promote preventive care. Lula highlighted government efforts to expand health access, such as health caravans reaching remote areas to reduce queues in the public system.

item.title: Tarcísio emplaca nomes do próprio governo em agências reguladoras após reforma para blindar órgãos
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) appointed Diego Domingues to lead a state regulatory agency, furthering his practice of placing government allies in key oversight roles following a reform designed to shield these bodies from political interference. The move strengthens executive control over regulatory agencies, sparking debate about institutional independence and the potential politicization of oversight bodies.

item.title: Até piadas são aprovadas nas adaptações de musicais da Broadway no Brasil; saiba como funciona
item.description: Adaptations of Broadway and West End musicals in Brazil have evolved from exact replicas to versions with a Brazilian touch, as noted by directors Tadeu Aguiar and Almali Zraik. Productions like 'Diana - The People's Princess' and 'Shrek' exemplify this trend, integrating local themes and social reflections beyond pure entertainment. The Brazilian market for foreign musicals solidified since the turn of the century, with growing preference for adaptations that resonate with national audiences.

item.title: Cármen diverge de Fux sobre prazo de 24 horas para eleição indireta no RJ
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Cármen Lúcia diverged from Justice Luiz Fux on the 24-hour disqualification deadline for indirect elections in Rio de Janeiro. She voted to uphold the deadline, while Fux argued it is manifestly insufficient to ensure electoral equality. The ruling affects the gubernatorial election following Cláudio Castro's resignation, with the Rio de Janeiro Tribunal of Justice calling a new vote.

item.title: Justiça do Rio anula sessão que elegeu Douglas Ruas para a presidência da Alerj
item.description: The Rio de Janeiro Court of Justice annulled the legislative session that elected Douglas Ruas as president of the State Assembly, following an injunction by Judge Suely Lopes Magalhães. The ruling stems from the TSE’s order to retotalize the 2022 election votes after the cassation of former president Rodrigo Bacellar for political and economic abuse and illicit resource collection. Ruas, an ally of Flávio Bolsonaro, was elected with 45 of 46 votes amid opposition protests over lack of prior notice.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro ironiza Lula após resposta à fala sobre "Opala velho"
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro mocked President Lula after Lula responded to his earlier comparison of Lula to an 'old Opala', saying the 'Opala drowned in alcohol' and that Lula was 'being Laura too much'. The exchange occurred during the opening of the Federative Caravan in Niterói, where Lula rebutted by stating that the Opala Flávio knows is his father Jair Bolsonaro's, which 'is in the junkyard'.

item.title: Lula nega ter clone
item.description: President Lula (PT) denied conspiracy theories claiming the existence of his 'clones' during an event in Rio de Janeiro, stating he is already 'four clones' and rebutting questions about his physical condition. He criticized the allegations spreading on social media and defended his authenticity.

item.title: CNJ e CNMP criam grupo para implementar novas regras para ‘penduricalhos’
item.description: The CNJ and CNMP have created a joint working group to monitor the implementation of the STF's decision limiting 'penduricalhos' (extra payments) to magistrates and prosecutors to 35% of their base salary, currently R$46,300. The group aims to ensure uniform application of the rules and strengthen transparency and oversight in subsidy payments, as Congress has not yet passed new legislation due to the electoral year.

item.title: CPMI do INSS: relatório do governo Lula culpa gestão Bolsonaro e cita 170 envolvidos
item.description: A parallel report from the INSS CPMI, drafted by Deputy Paulo Pimenta (PT-RS), accuses the Bolsonaro administration of enabling a scheme of improper deductions affecting beneficiaries, naming 170 individuals, including former ministers and INSS directors. The document highlights eight fraud nuclei and points to omissions by the Bolsonaro government, as well as normative acts that removed control barriers. Although some accused also served under Lula, the report focuses on the prior administration’s responsibility, citing donations received by Onyx Lorenzoni and activities of his son at Unibap, a company linked to the scheme.

item.title: Lula fala sobre gastos com cachorros em evento com empresários chineses
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva made a gaffe by mentioning expenses on dogs during an event with Chinese businessmen at the CAOA factory in Anápolis, Goiás. The comment was made on Thursday, the 26th, and drew criticism for being inappropriate in a diplomatic and business context. The original post was published on the O Antagonista website.

item.title: Cármen Lúcia diverge de Fux e vota para manter prazo para desincompatibilização em eleição no Rio
item.description: Minister Cármen Lúcia of the STF voted to maintain the 24-hour incompatibility deadline for the indirect election in Rio de Janeiro, partially diverging from Fux, arguing the rule is necessary due to the exceptional double vacancy and unpredictable election date. She agreed with Fux on suspending the open voting requirement in ALERJ, protecting deputies' freedom of conviction. Her vote relied on the PGR, TSE precedents, and the need to broaden candidate participation under institutional urgency.

item.title: Sem peruca e batom, Adrilles detona Flávio Bolsonaro, Erika Hilton e Lei da misoginia
item.description: Councilman Adrilles Jorge harshly criticized Flavio Bolsonaro, Erika Hilton, and the Misogyny Law during an interview on Papo Antagonista, using direct language without metaphorical 'makeup' to emphasize his stance.

item.title: Zema defende impeachment de Moraes e Toffoli
item.description: Governor Romeu Zema criticized the Supreme Federal Court (STF) and defended the impeachment of ministers Alexandre de Moraes and Dias Toffoli during an interview on Papo Antagonista. He stated that, if elected president of Brazil, he would make changes to the judiciary. The remarks were made amid a polarized political climate.

item.title: Zema critica STF por barrar a CPI do INSS: “Escândalo”
item.description: Romeu Zema (Novo), a pre-candidate for the 2026 Brazilian presidency, called the STF's decision to overturn Justice André Mendonça's injunction requiring Congress to extend the INSS CPMI a "scandal." He accused the Supreme Court justices of being "untouchables" working against Brazil and vowed to "end their party." The ruling (8-2) held that only the Senate president can extend the CPMI, with Mendonça and Fux dissenting.

item.title: Cármen, Mendonça e Nunes Marques divergem de Fux e defendem prazo de 24h para desincompatibilização nas eleições do Rio
item.description: Supreme Court ministers André Mendonça, Nunes Marques and Minister Cármen Lúcia voted to suspend the requirement for open voting in the indirect elections for Rio de Janeiro’s government, but diverged from rapporteur Luiz Fux by supporting a 24-hour deadline for candidate disengagement, opposing Fux’s six-month proposal. The virtual plenary judgment is set to conclude on Monday (30), with six other ministers yet to vote.

item.title: Oposição critica STF por derrubar prorrogação da CPI do INSS
item.description: Opposition to Lula's government criticized the Supreme Court's decision to overturn Justice André Mendonça's injunction to extend the INSS CPI, claiming it aimed to protect the president's son, Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), under fiscal secrecy investigation. Lawmakers such as Nikolas Ferreira, Carol de Toni, and Tereza Cristina accused the Court of blocking the truth and shielding itself from congressional oversight. The majority ruled that only the Senate President, Davi Alcolumbre, has the authority to extend the CPI.

item.title: Lula diz que é “Opala turbinado” e que Bolsonaro está “no desmanche”
item.description: Lula responded to Flávio Bolsonaro's criticism by calling himself a 'turbocharged Opala' and saying Jair Bolsonaro is 'in the scrapyard', during the Federative Caravan in Niterói. He stated the 2026 election will be a battle between truth and lies, condemning social media disinformation and emphasizing his physical fitness at age 80. Lula also noted that Jair Bolsonaro is hospitalized with bronchopneumonia and expected to be discharged soon.

item.title: STF tem 3 votos por prazo de 24h para desincompatibilização em eleição indireta no RJ
item.description: Three Supreme Court justices (Cármen Lúcia, André Mendonça and Kassio Nunes Marques) partially diverged from rapporteur Luiz Fux by suspending the requirement for candidates to disengage from public office 24 hours before the indirect election for governor in Rio de Janeiro. They argue that maintaining the 180-day disengagement period would exclude legitimate candidates who do not know in advance when a vacancy will occur. The virtual session judgment, expected to conclude on Monday, March 30, 2026, could alter the electoral landscape, as potential candidates like Douglas Rios and André Ceciliano currently hold public offices and would need to disengage.

item.title: DropsTec 26/03/2026 | Sua coletânea diária de notícias sobre tecnologia
item.description: Xiaomi announced a restructuring of the POCO brand in Brazil, targeting the premium smartphone segment. The move aims to compete directly with brands like Samsung and Apple in the high-end market. The news was featured in DropsTec on 03/26/2026, ToqueTec's daily tech roundup.

item.title: Lula diz que Rio “não tem governador” e critica gestão Castro
item.description: Lula claims Rio de Janeiro 'has no governor' following Claudio Castro's removal, criticizing police operations in favelas and arguing that organized crime must be fought by targeting leaders, not the poor. He states his government has transferred more resources to states regardless of political alignment and defends the Anti-Facction Law as a means to shift investigative focus 'upstream'.

item.title: STF chamou Lavareda para curar as feridas, mas não tem analista, propaganda ou mandinga que dê jeito
item.description: After the Master scandal, the STF consulted political analyst Antonio Lavareda, who advised ministers to speak less and engage more with independent voters. Despite efforts like Bolsonaro's house arrest and attempts to curb excessive salaries, the Court's actions were seen as inadequate patches, with 60% of Brazilians lacking trust in the STF and only André Mendonça having net positive approval.

item.title: “A resposta está nas urnas”, diz presidente da CPMI do INSS
item.description: Senator Carlos Viana (Podemos-MG), president of the INSS CPMI, lamented the STF ruling that overturned Minister André Mendonça’s decision granting 48 hours to Senate President Davi Alcolumbre (União-AP) to extend the committee’s deadline. Viana called the outcome a "cruel portrait of...", implying that the solution to INSS issues must come from the 2026 elections.

item.title: Lula e Alckmin se vacinam; governo inicia campanha da gripe
item.description: President Lula and Vice President Alckmin participated in the launch of the National Influenza Vaccination Campaign in Anápolis, where they were vaccinated and emphasized the importance of free immunization through UBS. The campaign begins this Saturday (March 28, 2026) in several regions, prioritizing children, pregnant women, and the elderly, with a 100% Brazilian vaccine developed in partnership with the Butantan Institute. The government will distribute 15.7 million doses and send 10 million institutional messages to boost outreach amid rising respiratory virus cases in 2026.

item.title: Força-tarefa encontra aumento de até 270% e autua 16 postos de combustíveis
item.description: The federal task force monitoring fuel prices, conducted from March 9 to March 25, 2026, fined 16 distributors for abusive pricing practices, with margins reaching up to 270% weekly in some cases. A total of 3,181 fuel stations and 236 distributors were inspected across 190 municipalities, generating 1,785 notifications. Led by Senacon and ANP with state Procons and the Federal Highway Police, the operation aimed to shield consumers from unjustified price hikes tied to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: CPI do INSS: após revés no STF, Viana quer votar relatório nesta sexta
item.description: Senator Carlos Viana (Podemos-MG) plans to read and vote on the final report of the INSS CPMI this Friday (27), after the STF rejected the investigation's extension by an 8-2 vote. The report, spanning about 5,000 pages, recommends indicting 228 individuals, including banker Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master, over alleged irregular consigned loan schemes targeting retirees. The CPMI, launched in August 2025, investiges undue deductions from INSS benefits.

item.title: Lula diz que governo pretende capturar os ‘bandidos do andar de cima’
item.description: President Lula stated that his government aims to capture the "high-level criminals" of organized crime, criticing operations that focus only on favelas and the poor. He emphasized the need to arrest criminals living in high-end condominiums and mentioned cooperation with the U.S. to extradite Brazilians involved in crime who reside in Miami. Lula also referenced Donald Trump's request to contribute to the extradition of Brazilian citizens linked to drug trafficking.

item.title: Após Flávio chamar Lula de 'Opala velhão', petista rebate e diz que Bolsonaro 'está no desmanche'
item.description: President Lula retaliated against Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's 'old Opala' comment by saying he once owned a turbocharged 1994 Opala and that Bolsonaro's 'Opala' — referring to Jair Bolsonaro — is 'in the scrapyard'. He made the remarks during the launch of the Federative Caravan in Niterói, where he also promoted the Anti-Criminal Organization Law and urged men to overcome fear of prostate exams.

item.title: Governo Lula assina decreto que reestrutura Coaf com objetivo de fortalecer combate ao crime organizado
item.description: The Lula government issued a decree to restructure the Coaf (Financial Activities Control Council), aiming to strengthen the fight against organized crime and financial crimes. The measure seeks to expand the agency’s reach and effectiveness following incidents that exposed weaknesses in the financial monitoring system. The reform reflects the government’s effort to enhance transparency and security in combating money laundering and terrorist financing.

item.title: “Intocáveis de sempre contra os cidadãos de bem”, diz Zema sobre STF
item.description: Former Minas Gerais governor Romeu Zema (Novo) criticized the STF for overturning a decision that gave 48 hours to investigate alleged theft from retirees, claiming the 'untouchables' of the Court ordered the investigation to stop. He said this represents an attack on 'decent citizens' and reinforced his narrative of fighting impunity. The video was posted on X (formerly Twitter).

item.title: Lula diz não se ofender por ser chamado de Opala por Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) responded with humor to Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL-RJ) comment calling him an 'Opala', stating that Jair Bolsonaro 'is in the junkyard'. Lula said he was not offended by the nickname and used it to mock the former president's political situation. The remarks were made during a public event on Thursday, the 26th.

item.title: Lula rebate Flávio por comparação com Opala: ‘é o pai dele, que está no desmanche’
item.description: President Lula rebutted Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's comparison of him to an 'old Opala', saying he is not offended by the analogy but that Flávio's father, Jair Bolsonaro, is the one 'in the junkyard'. Lula noted he once owned a turbocharged 1994 Opala and criticized the Bolsonaro family's management of federal hospitals in Rio, declaring this the 'year of truth' in the battle between truth and lies.

item.title: Papo Antagonista: Antagonista entrevista pré-candidatos à Presidência: Romeu Zema
item.description: Minas Gerais Governor Romeu Zema was interviewed on Papo Antagonista about his projects for Brazil's future, highlighting his views as a potential presidential candidate. The segment also covered the activities of councilman Adrilles Jorge, including the incident where he wore a wig and lipstick during an Alesp session.

item.title: “Pai dele que está em desmanche”, diz Lula a Flávio
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva countered Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's remark calling him an 'old Opala', stating instead that former President Jair Bolsonaro is the one 'in junkyard'. The exchange of insults took place during an event in Rio de Janeiro on Thursday, March 26. Lula turned the old car metaphor against Flávio's father, reversing the accusation.

item.title: Lula destaca relação de parceria entre governo federal e prefeituras
item.description: President Lula emphasized the importance of partnership between the federal government and municipal authorities to solve local problems during the opening of the 18th Federative Caravan in Rio de Janeiro. He stressed that national success depends on well-functioning cities and announced deliveries of dental units, equipment kits, and ambulances for municipalities in the state. The mayor of Niterói praised the federal government’s democratic and collaborative approach, contrasting it with authoritarian trends in Latin America.

item.title: Por 8 votos a 2, Supremo derruba prorrogação da CPMI do INSS
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court overturned, by a vote of 8 to 2, the extension of the INSS CPMI ordered by Minister André Mendonça, setting the conclusion of the commission's work for Saturday (28). The commission's president, Senator Carlos Viana (Podemos-MG), expects the final report to be read and voted on Friday (27). The CPMI was established in August 2025 to investigate undue deductions from retirees' and pensioners' INSS benefits.

item.title: “CNN Brasil” nomeia João Vitor Xavier como novo CEO
item.description: CNN Brasil appointed journalist and radio host João Vitor Xavier as its new CEO, announced on Thursday, March 26, 2026. Xavier, who also leads Rádio Itatiaia — the country’s top-rated radio station — will hold both positions following an invitation from majority shareholder Rubens Menin. He confirmed he will not run for re-election in 2026 to focus solely on managing the two media outlets, emphasizing his commitment to democracy and public information in Brazil.

item.title: Versão ‘moderada’ de Flávio já encomenda primeira crise com ala radical do bolsonarismo
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro, the PL presidential candidate, faced backlash from the radical wing of Bolsonarismo for supporting a bill that equates misogyny with racism, which conservatives view as a left-leaning proposal. Despite internal opposition, his moderate stance may be a strategic move to appeal to conservative voters not aligned with radical Bolsonarismo. He currently ties with Lula in voter intention polls, surprising even his most optimistic supporters.

item.title: Cortejado pelo PSB, Pacheco só definirá filiação no limite da janela
item.description: Senator Rodrigo Pacheco (PSD-MG) will wait until the party-switching window closes on April 3 to decide his new party, aiming to run for governor of Minas Gerais. He had dinner with PSB national president João Campos in Brasília, signaling interest in the party, but is still awaiting potential offers from MDB and União Brasil, which could provide more TV time and municipal infrastructure. Pacheco must leave the PSD because current vice-governor Mateus Simões, also from PSD and allied with Governor Romeu Zema, blocks his candidacy under that party.

item.title: Os sete recados do STF ao Congresso e ao próprio tribunal no julgamento que encerrou a CPI do INSS
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) rejected the extension of the INSS CPI, setting clear limits on parliamentary inquiries and reinforcing the separation of powers by stating that the duration of CPIs is the responsibility of Congress. Ministers also indicated that INSS frauds should be investigated by the Judiciary to avoid political exploitation in an election year, and protected Justices Alexandre de Moraes and Dias Toffoli from inquiries related to the Banco Master scandal.

item.title: Pesquisa expõe mudança no perfil histórico de apoio a Lula e traz sinal de alerta na campanha
item.description: A recent AtlasIntel poll shows 72% of Brazilians aged 16 to 24 disapprove of President Lula, marking a historic reversal in his electoral base, which once relied strongly on youth support. Approval is now concentrated among voters aged 45 to 59, highlighting a structural shift in Lula’s coalition. Analysts argue that the right’s superior digital communication strategy on social media has enabled young people to become opinion multipliers, amplifying their political influence beyond direct voting.

item.title: Entenda a decisão e os próximos passos da extradição de Carla Zambelli
item.description: On Thursday (26), the Italian Justice authorized the extradition of former deputy Carla Zambelli (PL) so she can serve a prison sentence in Brazil. The decision responds to a request from the Brazilian Judiciary, seeking to enforce a final conviction already ruled against the lawmaker.

item.title: Lula fala em medo de “dedada” e cobra exame de próstata de homens
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva urged men to undergo prostate exams, criticizing the fear of the 'digital rectal exam' and emphasizing cancer prevention. He said he plans to accompany First Lady Janja Lula da Silva in her routine check-ups to set an example. The remarks were made during the Caravana Federativa in Niterói, where the government also announced health investments, including ambulances, dental units, and funding for specialist programs.

item.title: ‘Funk do 01’ usado por Flávio não é propaganda eleitoral antecipada, diz advogado
item.description: Lawyer Vitor Barretta states that the use of the funk 'Zero Um, Novo Capitão' by Flávio Bolsonaro does not constitute early electoral propaganda, as there is no explicit or implicit request for votes. The song’s use in his pre-campaign is legal and aims to broaden his appeal beyond the Bolsonarist base.

item.title: Lula diz não se ofender por ser chamado de Opala por Flávio e fala que Bolsonaro ‘está no desmanche’
item.description: President Lula responded to Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's comparison of him to an 'old Opala', saying he is not offended and that Bolsonaro 'is in the junkyard', referring to his imprisonment and hospitalization. Lula also defended his health and fitness at 80, criticized the Bolsonaro family's management of federal hospitals in Rio de Janeiro, and attacked former governor Cláudio Castro.

item.title: STF forma maioria para derrubar liminar de Mendonça pela prorrogação da CPI mista do INSS
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) formed a majority to overturn Minister André Mendonça's injunction that had extended the mixed INSS CPI's work by 120 days, halting investigations into billion-dollar fraud schemes involving deductions from retirees' and pensioners' benefits. The ruling was supported by ministers Flávio Dino, Alexandre de Moraes, Nunes Marques, Cristiano Zanin, Dias Toffoli and Cármen Lúcia, opposed only by Mendonça and Luiz Fux. The CPI had been probing potential links involving Fábio Luís Lula da Silva and banker Daniel Vorcaro, who is in preventive detention and negotiating a plea deal.

item.title: Gilmar Mendes diz que vazamento de mensagens de Vorcaro foi “criminoso”
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Gilmar Mendes labeled the leak of Daniel Vorcaro's messages as criminal and abominable, stating that unjustified breach of secrecy is unconstitutional and illegal. He made the remarks during the ruling that blocked the extension of the INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee, addressing senators Carlos Viana and Alfredo Gaspar, who were present in the plenary. Justice Alexandre de Moraes agreed, while Minister André Mendonça asserted that decisions were properly executed and emphasized the need to investigate the leak, which hinders the investigation into Vorcaro's alleged misconduct.

item.title: Lula cita gastos com cães e diz que chineses não têm esse problema
item.description: President Lula stated that pet-related expenses in Brazil have risen over the decades and now constitute a 'seizure of salary,' citing costs such as pet dentistry, weekly baths, and veterinary care. He contrasted this with the alleged absence of such expenses among Chinese people, a remark that may be seen as prejudiced due to its reliance on a stereotype. Lula also urged Finance Minister Dario Durigan to address Brazilian household debt, proposing financial literacy campaigns on television.

item.title: Eleição na Alerj cria mais um nó jurídico e sucessão no Rio depende de tribunal
item.description: The election for the presidency of the Rio de Janeiro State Legislature (Alerj) has sparked judicial challenges from the opposition, who argue the process was illegal due to insufficient notice and lack of genuine debate. Douglas Ruas, a pre-candidate for governor in 2026, was elected with 45 of 70 votes and may assume interim control of the Guanabara Palace if the ruling stands. The PL is positioning Ruas as a candidate in the indirect elections to complete Cláudio Castro’s term, pending a Supreme Federal Court (STF) decision on the electoral rules.
